channel.title: Comments on: Episode 31 – Oskar Blues Ten FIDY Imperial Stout
channel.description: Beer America TV is the first beer web site dedicated to the specialty craft brewing world with video on industry experts and insiders plus more!

item.title: By: Jim Powers
item.dc:creator: Jim Powers
item.description:
Dale's Ale rocked our world, lads! hope you never go thirsty and let Beercompass help keep you hydrated with the finest brew: itunes app store search term: beercompass
enjoy! Jim beercompassapp@gmail.com
item.content:encoded:
Dale’s Ale rocked our world, lads! hope you never go thirsty and let Beercompass help keep you hydrated with the finest brew: itunes app store search term: beercompass

enjoy!
Jim
beercompassapp@gmail.com

item.title: By: Chipper Dave
item.dc:creator: Chipper Dave
item.description: Oskar Blues keeps on expanding. I bet one day soon they'll be available in almost every state. Their web site has a nice virtual tour. I decided to take my own tour of their new Longmont facility and got a nice guided tour. Ten Fidy is also available aged in whiskey barrels at their restaurant in Lyons. I saw their barrels in their warehouse and was drooling as the prospect of trying that. I suggest if you can ever do this - try aging the Ten Fidy for a year. Aging does wonders to a big beer like this. I've kept one can of Ten Fidy since last February and am patiently awaiting the anniversary date to open it up again. Stock up on this as it tends to go quickly. A bit pricey but worth it.
item.content:encoded: Oskar Blues keeps on expanding. I bet one day soon they’ll be available in almost every state. Their web site has a nice virtual tour. I decided to take my own tour of their new Longmont facility and got a nice guided tour. Ten Fidy is also available aged in whiskey barrels at their restaurant in Lyons. I saw their barrels in their warehouse and was drooling as the prospect of trying that. I suggest if you can ever do this – try aging the Ten Fidy for a year. Aging does wonders to a big beer like this. I’ve kept one can of Ten Fidy since last February and am patiently awaiting the anniversary date to open it up again. Stock up on this as it tends to go quickly. A bit pricey but worth it.
